text_original: Now arms, however beautiful, are instruments of evil omen, hateful, it may be said, to all creatures.
text_normalized: Now arms, however beautiful, are instruments of evil omen, hateful, it may be said, to all creatures.

text_original: Therefore they who have the Dao do not like to employ them.
text_normalized: Therefore they who have the Dao do not like to employ them.

text_original: The superior man ordinarily considers the left hand the most honourable place, but in time of war the right hand.
text_normalized: The superior man ordinarily considers the left hand the most honourable place, but in time of war the right hand.

text_original: Those sharp weapons are instruments of evil omen, and not the instruments of the superior man;
text_normalized: Those sharp weapons are instruments of evil omen, and not the instruments of the superior man;

text_original: - he uses them only on the compulsion of necessity.
text_normalized: - he uses them only on the compulsion of necessity.

text_original: Calm and repose are what he prizes;
text_normalized: Calm and repose are what he prizes;

text_original: victory (by force of arms) is to him undesirable.
text_normalized: victory (by force of arms) is to him undesirable.

text_original: To consider this desirable would be to delight in the slaughter of men;
text_normalized: To consider this desirable would be to delight in the slaughter of men;

text_original: and he who delights in the slaughter of men cannot get his will in the kingdom.
text_normalized: and he who delights in the slaughter of men cannot get his will in the kingdom.

text_original: On occasions of festivity to be on the left hand is the prized position;
text_normalized: On occasions of festivity to be on the left hand is the prized position;

text_original: on occasions of mourning, the right hand.
text_normalized: on occasions of mourning, the right hand.

text_original: The second in command of the army has his place on the left;
text_normalized: The second in command of the army has his place on the left;

text_original: the general commanding in chief has his on the right;
text_normalized: the general commanding in chief has his on the right;

text_original: - his place, that is, is assigned to him as in the rites of mourning.
text_normalized: - his place, that is, is assigned to him as in the rites of mourning.

text_original: He who has killed multitudes of men should weep for them with the bitterest grief;
text_normalized: He who has killed multitudes of men should weep for them with the bitterest grief;

text_original: and the victor in battle has his place (rightly) according to those rites.
text_normalized: and the victor in battle has his place (rightly) according to those rites.
